subject: Statistics
title: Finding Lost Objects with Bayes
description: A micro-lesson that makes Finding Lost Objects with Bayes usable.

hook.text: In 2009, Bayesian search found Air France Flight 447's wreckage in the vast Atlantic Ocean after two years.

buildup.text: Searchers divided the ocean into grid cells and assigned each a prior probability of containing the wreckage.

discovery.text: Each failed search updated the probabilities. Areas searched and found empty shifted probability to unsearched zones.

twist.text: The wreckage was found exactly where the Bayesian model predicted — a spot initially given low probability.

climax.text: The same method found the Scorpion submarine in 1968 and the San Jose shipwreck in 2015.

punchline.text: Bayes finds needles in oceans. Literally.

quiz.question: How did Bayesian search help find Air France Flight 447?
options: Failed searches updated probabilities, eventually pointing to the correct location
It predicted the crash location immediately
It used satellite imagery to scan the entire ocean
